quoteLast: , when the longitude, by the time-keeper, was 189° 24', it extended from S. 72° W. to N. 41° E. Between the S.W. extreme, and a point which bore W., two leagues distant, the shore forms a large bay, in which we anchored at ten o'clock in the forenoon, about two miles from the north shore, in ten fathoms water, over a gravelly bottom.
title: A General History and Collection of Voyages and Travels, Volume 16
